meta.reportLabel: Tue Report — RBNZ T-minus hours: ~75-80% hike priced for tonight 02:00 UTC (22/28 economists hike; ASB/Westpac flipped to hold on the oil slide; all majors see Sept hikes regardless - a hold delays, not cancels); USD steadied on the in-line ISM (54.0, prices 67.7 first sub-70 since Feb) - DXY back >101 into Wed's Fed minutes; USD/JPY grinds to ~162.3 DEEPER in the MoF zone, verbal-only pushback; WTI ~$68.6.
meta.coverage: USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, AUD, NZD, CAD · 18 pairs + DXY + JPYBASKET + GER40 + XAU + XAG + USOIL
meta.regime: RBNZ T-minus hours: ~75-80% of a hike is priced for Wednesday 02:00 UTC with the street split (22/28 hike; ASB/Westpac flipped to hold on cheap oil) - the HOLD is the pain trade, and every major bank sees hikes from September even on a hold. The USD steadied on the in-line ISM (DXY back >101) into Wednesday's stale-by-construction Fed minutes; USD/JPY grinds deeper into the proven MoF zone (~162.3) on verbal-only pushback; the oil glut (WTI ~$68.6) keeps CAD weakest. US-Iran talks resume Jul 11.
meta.nextBigEvent: Wed Jul 8, 02:00 UTC: RBNZ - the binary (~75-80% hike priced); then Fed June minutes 18:00 UTC

dailyRead: RBNZ T-minus hours: the week's coin-flip lands Wednesday 02:00 UTC with OIS pricing ~75-80% of a hike to 2.50% (22/28 economists say hike; ASB and Westpac flipped to hold on the oil slide; ANZ's base case is a deliberately 'neutral-to-dovish' hike) - and crucially, all the major banks see hikes from September even if Wednesday is a hold, so a hold is a delay, not a cancellation. The USD steadied: ISM Services printed 54.0 in line (prices paid 67.7, first sub-70 since February - a disinflationary wrinkle; employment 51.2, first expansion in four months - softening the NFP-miss panic), pushing DXY back above 101 ahead of Wednesday's Fed June minutes. USD/JPY ground to ~162.3, deeper into the proven MoF zone with only verbal pushback after the record Y11.73tn ($74bn) April-May round; the oil glut holds WTI ~$68.6 near five-month lows, keeping CAD the weakest currency. Cleanest board unchanged: USD/CAD and AUD/CAD longs; NZD is an event trade only until Wednesday.

today.headline: RBNZ T-minus hours — ~75-80% hike priced for tonight 02:00 UTC
today.read: The HOLD is the pain trade, but even the base-case hike can sell off on ANZ's 'neutral-to-dovish' script - and every major bank sees Sept hikes regardless, so a hold delays rather than cancels. No pre-positioning edge; NZD is event-only until the statement.

today.headline: ISM Services 54.0 in line — the post-NFP slide stopped, DXY back >101
today.read: Prices paid 67.7 (first sub-70 since Feb) is a disinflationary wrinkle but employment 51.2 back expanding softens the NFP panic; Sept holds ~50%. Wednesday's minutes are stale by construction - the Jul 14 CPI is still the arbiter.

today.headline: USD/JPY grinds to ~162.3 — deeper in the MoF zone, verbal-only pushback
today.read: Above April's trigger with the Y11.73tn precedent and no intervention yet - the ambush lottery gets richer with every tick higher. Defend, don't add; a no-warning strike is the tail on every XXX/JPY position.

today.headline: No change — WTI ~$68.6 near 5-month lows, glut holds
today.read: Still the weakest currency; USD/CAD and AUD/CAD longs stay the cleanest board. Friday's June jobs (was May's +88k noise?) is the near catalyst before BoC Jul 15.

today.headline: No change — Aug hike tail stays thin (~36%, 'outlier')
today.read: The sticky-core floor stands but the near-term kicker is gone; the AUD/CAD long leans on the weak CAD leg until the Q2 CPI late July.

today.headline: No change — soft with a hawkish wrinkle
today.read: Flash 2.8% cooling guts Jul 23 urgency, but core at 2.5% keeps a Sept hike alive. The USD leg drives EUR/USD; the wrinkle is the squeeze risk.

today.headline: No change — re-armed BoE thesis holds
today.read: Services CPI 3.7% keeps both hike dissents alive into Jul 30. Firm, rangebound vs USD.

strength.tag: ISM in-line; DXY >101; minutes Wed, CPI Jul 14
strength.drivers: CPI 4.2% headline / core 2.9% (May); June NFP +57k big miss but ISM services employment 51.2 back expanding; ISM prices 67.7 first sub-70 since Feb; Fed 3.50-3.75% hawkish hold, zero 2026 cuts priced; Sept hike ~50%.
strength.forward: 🦅⚖️ Hawkish hold into the Jul 28-29 FOMC; the in-line ISM stopped the post-NFP slide and Sept holds ~50%. Wednesday's minutes are stale by construction (pre-NFP); the Jun CPI (Jul 14) re-arms or kills the hike tail. Risk: a second soft print in a row turns the crack into a trend. Next FOMC Jul 28-29.

strength.drivers: Core 2.6% (May, up from 2.5%); services CPI 3.7% (up from 3.2%); headline 2.8% held; DMP wages 4.2%. BoE 3.75%, hawkish 7-2 hold (2 hike votes); quiet tape, thesis unchanged.
strength.forward: 🦅 Hawkish - the services re-acceleration keeps the hike tail and both dissents alive into the Jul 30 MPR round. Risk: a soft activity/labor wave re-splits the MPC. Next BoE Jul 30.

strength.drivers: CPI 3.1% Q1 (RBNZ sees >4% Q2); unemp 5.3% Q1. RBNZ 2.25%; May was a 3-3 split decided by Governor Breman. OIS ~75-80% for a Jul 8 hike; 22/28 economists expect 2.50% but ASB/Westpac flipped to hold on the oil slide; all majors see hikes from Sept regardless.
strength.forward: 🦅 Hike is the base case tonight but the street is split and ANZ's script is a 'neutral-to-dovish' hike - raise, then sound noncommittal; a hold delays rather than cancels (Sept consensus). Risk: a HOLD flushes the 75-80% premium hard; even a delivered hike can sell off on dovish guidance. Next RBNZ Jul 8 (tonight).

strength.drivers: May CPI: trimmed mean 3.6% (up from 3.4%); headline 4.0%; unemp 4.5% loosening. RBA 4.35% (held Jun after 75bp of 2026 hikes). The Aug 11 hike is priced only ~36% and increasingly called an outlier; Dec ~67%.
strength.forward: ⚖️ The sticky-core floor holds but the near-term hawkish kicker faded - RBA seen on hold through Q3. Risk: a hot Q2 CPI (late Jul) re-arms August; risk-off flushes the commodity beta. Next RBA Aug 11.

strength.drivers: Core CPI 1.4% (May, subsidy-suppressed); unemp 2.5%, jobs/applicants 1.26 tight. BoJ 1.00% (hiked Jun 16, 7-1), guides more hikes. Spot ground to ~162.3 - above April's trigger - with only verbal warnings after the record Y11.73tn ($74bn) April-May round.
strength.forward: 🦅⚖️ Deeper in the proven MoF zone with every tick; the trade stays an ambush lottery, not momentum; the BoJ path (Jul 31) stays hawkish. Risk: for JPY shorts a no-warning strike; for JPY longs the ~250bp+ carry bleed with no catalyst date. Next BoJ Jul 31.

strength.tag: flash 2.8% cooling BUT core up to 2.5%
strength.drivers: EZ June flash HICP 2.8% (down from 3.2%) BUT core ROSE to 2.5% from 2.2%. Unemp 6.3%. ECB 2.25% depo (hiked Jun 11, first since 2023), meeting-by-meeting.
strength.forward: ⚖️ The cooling headline guts Jul 23 urgency (hold expected) but rising core keeps a Sept hike alive - the dovish EUR read is not clean. Risk: a hawkish Jul 23 tone on the core print squeezes EUR shorts. Next ECB Jul 23.

strength.tag: WTI ~$68.6 near 5-mo lows; Fri jobs next
strength.drivers: May CPI 3.2% (energy-driven; ex-energy 2.1%, trim ~2.0%); unemp 6.6%; May +88k (best since Dec-24) but jobs flat YTD, wages 3.0% cooling; technical recession. BoC 2.25%, 5th straight hold. WTI ~$68.6; Saudi exports at 90% of pre-war baseline, Iranian crude stacking at sea.
strength.forward: 🔻 Glut intact - structurally offered; BoC sidelined Jul 15; Friday's June jobs print (whether May's +88k was noise) is the near catalyst. Risk: a Hormuz incident re-spikes oil; a second strong jobs print firms the floor. Next BoC Jul 15.

geopolitics: Funeral week enters its final stretch - and the calendar is set. Khamenei's rites run through July 9 (Tehran -> Qom -> burial at the Imam Reza Shrine in Mashhad), with the Doha talks scheduled to resume July 11; Kushner and Witkoff lead the US side in the indirect format, with Qatar and Pakistan mediating. The rhetoric around the vacuum is two-sided: an adviser to Iran's parliamentary speaker vowed 'revenge' during the ceremonies and Tehran issued a fresh warning that vessels must follow Iran-designated routes through the Strait of Hormuz, while Trump declared the US will win 'one way or the other.' The standing collision point is unchanged: the June 17 MoU obliges Iran to 'best efforts' toll-free Hormuz transit for 60 days only (lapsing ~mid-August), and Iranian officials keep repeating that fees will 'definitely' come - friendly nations get 'special treatment' - while Washington rejects the concept outright. Oil is not pricing this: WTI ~$68.6 near five-month lows, Brent ~$71-72, Saudi exports hold at 90% of the pre-war baseline, Hormuz traffic keeps recovering, Iranian crude stacking at sea, record Russian exports. Transmission: falling oil -> eroding energy-CPI floors -> less CB hawkishness at the margin - though Wellington is the live test of that logic tonight, with ASB and Westpac flipping to hold BECAUSE of cheaper fuel while 22/28 economists still say hike. CAD holds the bag; falling oil remains the GER40 tailwind and does NOT buy a Fed cut (US core 2.9%; the disinflation is energy-led). Through Thursday the tail is the funeral vacuum itself - an incident with negotiators sidelined would re-spike oil into a market that has crushed the war premium (rescuing CAD, hurting AUD/NZD/GER40, handing USD and JPY competing haven bids); from Saturday the tail migrates to the talks and the mid-August toll clock.
